data.narrative:
Space Forge is a Series A-stage space and aerospace company that has raised $40M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer universe of five same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private companies (Aalyria, Stoke Space, ClearSpace, Hubble Network, Sift) valued between $274M and $1.3B, alongside six public space and aerospace comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL, and one additional ticker).

Two valuation methods prove applicable. The stage-progression method, using total raised as a proxy base, applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A advancement, yielding $80M. The peer-median method anchors to the $400M median valuation across the five private comparables, reflecting mixed Series A and Series B stage companies in the same sector. The public-comp approach cannot be applied without Space Forge's revenue figures. These two methods triangulate to a median valuation of $240M, with a low of $68M and a high of $460M.

The wide range reflects material uncertainty around Space Forge's operational progress relative to the peer set and the heterogeneity within the comparable cohort—notably, Series B valuations (Aalyria at $1.3B, Stoke Space at $1.0B) sit substantially higher than Series A benchmarks. Key drivers of final valuation will be customer traction, technical milestones, and capital efficiency relative to peer trajectories.
